---
date: "2026-06-11"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    fontsize: 1.05em
    mainfont: "Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
library(estimatr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(kableExtra)
library(texreg)

Platinum <- read.csv("C:/Users/HP/Desktop/EGAP_Learning_Days/Platinum Data.csv")

dataa <- Platinum %>%
  rename(
    Y = Task.Completion.Rate..Y.,
    X = Did.you.get.deadline.reminder..X.
  )

fit <- lm_robust(Y ~ X, data = dataa)
```

```{css}
body { background: #f7f7f7; color: #1a1a1a; font-size: 1.05rem; }

.slide {
  background: #fff;
  border: 1px solid #e2e2e2;
  border-radius: 10px;
  overflow: hidden;
  margin-bottom: 28px;
}

.slide-header {
  background: #f0f0f0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e2e2e2;
  padding: 14px 28px;
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: 1.15rem;
  color: #1a1a1a;
}
.slide-num {
  font-size: .72rem;
  font-weight: 800;
  color: #999;
  letter-spacing: .9px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  margin-right: 10px;
}

.slide-body {
  padding: 26px 32px;
  font-size: 1rem;
  line-height: 1.7;
}

.two-col {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  gap: 28px;
}

.box {
  background: #f5f5f5;
  border-left: 3px solid #aaa;
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 14px 18px;
  margin: 10px 0;
  font-size: 1rem;
  line-height: 1.65;
}
.box-dark {
  background: #f0f0f0;
  border-left: 3px solid #555;
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 14px 18px;
  margin: 10px 0;
  font-size: 1rem;
  line-height: 1.65;
}

.section-label {
  font-size: .75rem;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: .8px;
  color: #888;
  font-weight: 700;
  margin: 16px 0 8px;
}

.badge {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 5px 13px;
  border-radius: 5px;
  font-size: .85rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  background: #ebebeb;
  color: #444;
  border: 1px solid #d0d0d0;
  margin: 3px;
}

.tag {
  display: inline-block;
  background: #1a1a1a;
  color: #fff;
  border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 2px 9px;
  font-size: .78rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  margin-right: 6px;
}

.title-slide {
  background: #1a1a1a;
  padding: 50px 36px;
  text-align: center;
  border-radius: 10px;
  margin-bottom: 28px;
}
.title-main {
  font-size: 1.7rem;
  font-weight: 800;
  color: #fff;
  line-height: 1.35;
  margin-bottom: 10px;
}
.title-sub {
  font-size: 1rem;
  color: #bbb;
  margin-bottom: 24px;
}
.title-label {
  font-size: .82rem;
  color: #888;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: .6px;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}
.author-pills {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 12px;
  margin: 20px 0 14px;
}
.author-pill {
  background: rgba(255,255,255,.1);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.2);
  border-radius: 20px;
  padding: 6px 20px;
  font-size: .92rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #fff;
}

.page-num {
  text-align: right;
  font-size: .72rem;
  color: #ccc;
  padding: 7px 20px;
  border-top: 1px solid #ebebeb;
}

/* Make kable table header black */
table.table thead tr th {
  background-color: #1a1a1a !important;
  color: #fff !important;
}

p { margin-bottom: 10px; }
li { margin-bottom: 8px; }
```

:::::: title-slide
::: {style="text-align:left;margin-bottom:18px;"}
<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="EGAP Logo" style="height:48px;opacity:.95;"/>
:::

<p class="title-label">

EGAP Learning Days 2026 · Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire

</p>

::: title-main
The Effect of Deadline Announcement<br>on Task Completion Rate
:::

<p class="title-sub">

Evidence from EGAP Learning Days Participants

</p>

::: author-pills
[Ouattara N Ben Idriss]{.author-pill} [Godwin UDDON]{.author-pill} [Godson NWANKWO]{.author-pill} [Davis Mawuena Aweso]{.author-pill} [Abdul-Hanan]{.author-pill}
:::

<p style="font-size:.82rem;color:#888;margin-top:8px;">

June 11, 2026 · EGAP Learning Days, Abidjan

</p>
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::::::::::: slide
::: slide-header
[SLIDE 2]{.slide-num} High-level question
:::

:::::::: slide-body
::::::: two-col
:::: {}
<p class="section-label">

Research Question

</p>

::: box
Does receiving a **deadline reminder** increase task **completion rates** among workshop participants?
:::
::::

:::: {}
<p class="section-label">

Proposed Answer

</p>

::: box-dark
We hypothesize that participants who **receive a deadline reminder** will be more likely to **complete the assigned task** than participants **who do not receive a reminder**.
:::
::::
:::::::
::::::::

::: page-num
2 / 7
:::
:::::::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

:::::: slide
::: slide-header
[SLIDE 3]{.slide-num} Research Design — iMIDA
:::

::: slide-body
[i · Inquiry (abstract)]{.badge} [M · Model]{.badge} [I · Inquiry (precise)]{.badge} [D · Data Strategy]{.badge} [A · Answer Strategy]{.badge}

**i —** How can simple behavioral interventions improve task completion?

Many individuals intend to complete assigned tasks but fail to do so because of forgetfulness, procrastination, or competing priorities. Understanding how to encourage follow-through is important for improving participation and productivity.

**M —** We propose that deadline reminders increase task completion by making the task more salient and reducing forgetting. Deadline Reminder –\> Greater Attention to the Task –\> Less Forgetting / Less Procrastination –\> Higher Task Completion

**I —** Does receiving a deadline reminder increase task completion rates among workshop participants?

**D —** Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT), coin-flip assignment. **Treatment Group** Receives a deadline reminder before the submission deadline.**Control Group** Receives no reminder. Outcome: binary completion (1 = submitted; 0 = not).

**A —** Estimate the causal effect of reminders by comparing completion rates between treatment and control groups. $ATE= \bar{Y}_T-\bar{Y}_C$
:::

::: page-num
3 / 7
:::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::::::::: slide
::: slide-header
[SLIDE 4]{.slide-num} Implementation Report
:::

:::::: slide-body
::::: two-col
::: {}
[WHAT]{.tag} We conducted a small randomized controlled trial (RCT) to evaluate whether receiving a deadline reminder increases the likelihood that workshop participants complete an assigned task.

[HOW]{.tag} Participants were randomly assigned to one of two groups:

**Treatment group**: received a deadline reminder before the submission deadline. **Control group**: did not receive any reminder.

After the deadline, we recorded whether each participant completed the task.

[TO WHOM]{.tag} The study was conducted among workshop participants.

[WHERE]{.tag} The experiment was implemented within the context of the workshop.
:::

::: {}
[WHEN]{.tag} The intervention was implemented during the workshop period

[WHY THIS WAY]{.tag} We used random assignment to ensure that the treatment and control groups were comparable. This design allows us to estimate the causal effect of deadline reminders on task completion while minimizing selection bias.

[WHAT WENT BADLY]{.tag} The sample size was relatively small, reducing statistical power.
:::
:::::
::::::

::: page-num
4 / 7
:::
:::::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

:::::::::: slide
::: slide-header
[SLIDE 5]{.slide-num} Answering Strategy
:::

::::::: slide-body
:::::: two-col
:::: {}
<p class="section-label">

Estimator

</p>

::: box-dark
**Difference-in-means (OLS):**

`Y = β₀ + β₁ · X + ε`

- Y = task completion (0 / 1)
- X = deadline treatment (0 = control, 1 = treated)
- **β₁ = estimated ATE**
:::
::::

::: {}
<p class="section-label">

Uncertainty Estimation

</p>

We report **95% confidence intervals** and **p-values.**
:::
::::::
:::::::

::: page-num
5 / 7
:::
::::::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::::::::::: slide
::: slide-header
[SLIDE 6]{.slide-num} Results — Regression Output
:::

:::::::: slide-body
```{r results-table, results='asis'}
# Construire l'objet texreg manuellement avec les SE robustes HC2 de lm_robust
tr <- createTexreg(
  coef.names = names(coef(fit)),
  coef       = coef(fit),
  se         = fit$std.error,
  pvalues    = fit$p.value,
  ci.low     = fit$conf.low,
  ci.up      = fit$conf.high,
  gof.names  = c("R<sup>2</sup>", "Adj. R<sup>2</sup>", "Num. obs.", "RMSE"),
  gof        = c(
    summary(lm(Y ~ X, data = dataa))$r.squared,
    summary(lm(Y ~ X, data = dataa))$adj.r.squared,
    fit$nobs,
    sqrt(mean(fit$residuals^2))
  ),
  gof.decimal = c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE)
)

htmlreg(
  list(tr),
  caption       = "lm_robust(Y ~ X, data = dataa) — HC2 Robust Standard Errors",
  caption.above = TRUE,
  single.row    = FALSE,
  stars         = c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05),
  custom.note   = "HC2 heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors in parentheses. %stars",
  custom.coef.names = c("(Intercept)", "X (Deadline)"),
  html.tag      = FALSE,
  head.tag      = FALSE,
  body.tag      = FALSE
)
```

::::::: two-col
:::: {}
::: box
**Intercept** = completion rate in the **control group** (no deadline announced).
:::
::::

:::: {}
::: box-dark
**Coefficient on X** = estimated ATE — the average effect of announcing a deadline on completion rate.
:::
::::
:::::::
::::::::

::: page-num
6 / 7
:::
:::::::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

:::::::::::::::: slide
::: slide-header
[SLIDE 7]{.slide-num} Interpretation, Threats & Takeaways
:::

:::::::: slide-body
:::::: two-col
::::: {}
<p class="section-label">

Interpretation

</p>

::: box-dark
### Findings

- Control group completion rate: **80%**

- Estimated treatment effect: **+20 percentage points**

- p-value: **0.152**

- 95% CI: **\[-0.08, 0.48\]**

**c/c** :Although participants who received a deadline reminder had a higher task completion rate, the estimated effect is not statistically significant (p = 0.152). Therefore, we fail to reject the null hypothesis that reminders have no effect on task completion.
:::

<p class="section-label" style="margin-top:16px;">

**Key Takeaway**

</p>

::: {}
<p class="section-label">

**Threats to Inference**

</p>

- **Low power** — DF = 17, too small to detect modest effects
- **Ceiling effect** — 80% control rate leaves little room to improve
- **Spillovers** — participants may have shared deadline info (SUTVA)
- **Hawthorne effect** — observation itself may have raised both groups' performance
:::
:::::
::::::

::: page-num
7 / 7
:::
::::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

:::::::: slide
::: slide-header
[SLIDE 8]{.slide-num} Thank You
:::

::::: slide-body
::: {style="text-align:center;margin-bottom:24px;"}
<p style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:800;margin-bottom:6px;">

Thank you for your attention!

</p>

<p style="font-size:1.1rem;color:#666;">

A special thank you to our instructors — we truly appreciate your guidance and dedication.

</p>
:::

::: {style="text-align:center;"}
<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Instructors photo" style="max-width:85%;border-radius:10px;box-shadow:0 4px 18px rgba(0,0,0,.13);"/>
:::

<p style="text-align:center;font-size:.85rem;color:#aaa;margin-top:14px;">

Vincent · Alyssa · Adikath · Yannick · Brice · Mac · Vin

</p>
:::::

::: page-num
8 / 8
:::
::::::::
::::::::::::::::
